narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A database that has been sized for a workload it doesn't have. CPU, connections, and IO wait are all flat low across all 14 days. The fix is a one-step instance-class downgrade.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests the Data Layer Analyst's ability to identify database over-provisioning from low-utilization signals across multiple metrics (not just CPU) and ground the recommendation in the current instance class.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Downsize the primary RDS instance from db.r6g.xlarge to db.r6g.large and enable storage auto-scaling. Expected savings: $1,680/month.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation says "downsize the database." A great recommendation specifies the from→to instance class, adds storage auto-scaling as a secondary improvement, and quotes the expected savings precisely.

before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: db_query_p95_latency_ms stayed under 100ms, db_connections_p95 stayed under 45 (well below new pool ceiling), SLA 99.7% preserved, monthly DB cost dropped by $1,680.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across analytics-backend cohort during Q4 2025.

target_recommendation.specific_change: Downsize primary RDS instance from db.r6g.xlarge to db.r6g.large and enable storage auto-scaling.
